principle: Number of clicks does not matter — difficulty per click does. Ten effortless clicks beat three confusing ones, so prefer multi-step flows with one simple choice per screen
explanation: X.ux|click_difficulty: 3_click_rule == myth; 10_easy_clicks >> 3_hard_clicks; hard_click: requires_reading_paragraphs + comparing_options + recalling_credentials; easy_click: instant_obvious_unambiguous; generator_rule: multi_step_wizard_1-2_fields_per_step >> single_form_12_fields; each_step == one_question_one_answer; NO:cramming_all_fields_on_one_page NO:limiting_navigation_depth_artificially NO:complex_choice_without_context eg: "checkout_one_page_15_fields" >> "checkout_5_steps_x_3_fields_each_with_progress_bar"

principle: When a choice is unavoidably complex place brief guidance right at the decision point — not on a help page, not in a tooltip the user might miss
explanation: X.ux|decision_guidance: complex_choice_needs_help_RIGHT_THERE; three_rules: brief (smallest_amount_of_info_that_helps) + timely (at_the_point_of_decision) + unavoidable (user_cant_miss_it); inline_hint_below_field >> help_icon_tooltip >> link_to_FAQ_page; eg: shipping_option: "standard_vs_express" + inline_text "arrives_by_friday" >> "see_shipping_policy_page"; NO:guidance_on_separate_page NO:help_text_user_must_click_to_reveal NO:long_explanatory_paragraphs_at_decision_point eg: "choose_plan_link_to_comparison_page" >> "choose_plan_with_one_line_benefit_under_each_option"
